channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Story of Y Bwthyn Bach, Queen Elizabeth II’s Childhood Cottage in Windsor Great Park
item.description: Y Bwthyn Bach, meaning “The Little House” in Welsh, was a miniature cottage gifted to Princess Elizabeth, the future Queen Elizabeth II, for her sixth birthday in 1932. The playhouse was presented by ...

item.title: Health experts raise concerns over heat, infectious diseases and food safety ahead of FIFA World Cup kickoff
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup scheduled to hold across the United States, Mexico, and Canada from June 11 to July 19 draws near, health experts and government officials are expressing concerns about possible ...

item.title: Arsenal win the 2025-26 Premier League title after dramatic season filled with managerial changes and late goals
item.description: The article reviews the fictional 2025-26 Premier League season month by month through a collection of memorable photographs and key moments. Liverpool began the campaign strongly despite the emotiona...

item.title: JD Vance Invites Republican Attorneys General Earlier Than Democrats for Fraud Task Force Meeting
item.description: Vice President JD Vance has been tasked by President Donald Trump with leading the administration's efforts to combat fraud. As part of this initiative, a White House meeting was scheduled for Tuesday...

item.title: The Knicks’ Winning Streak and the Danhausen ‘Curse’ Narrative Explained
item.description: The article explores a humorous and speculative sports storyline linking the New York Knicks’ recent winning streak to professional wrestler Danhausen and his self-described “curse.” The narrative beg...

item.title: Passengers evacuated from UK train after air conditioning failure during record-breaking heatwave
item.description: Passengers travelling on a Chiltern Railways service from London Marylebone to Stourbridge Junction were forced to disembark after the train’s air conditioning system failed during an exceptional heat...

item.title: Vintage Photographs Capture Daily Life at Chicago’s Riverview Amusement Park in 1962
item.description: The article highlights a collection of vintage photographs taken by photographer Laird Scott during the summer of 1962 at Chicago’s famous Riverview Amusement Park. Once considered one of the largest ...

item.title: Grigori Rasputin: Historical Portraits and His Role as Advisor to the Russian Imperial Family
item.description: Grigori Yefimovich Rasputin was a Russian mystic and faith healer born in 1869 in a poor Siberian peasant family. Known by nicknames such as the 'Mad Monk' or 'Holy Devil,' he rose to prominence as a ...

item.title: Leo Woodall and Dustin Hoffman shine in understated crime drama Tuner
item.description: The Guardian’s review of Tuner praises the chemistry between Leo Woodall and Dustin Hoffman in a restrained crime drama that blends elements of romance, comedy and thriller storytelling. Woodall plays...

item.title: Survey shows regional differences in Filipinos’ support for defending the West Philippine Sea, with notable opposition in Davao Region
item.description: An Octa Research survey commissioned by the Armed Forces of the Philippines reveals strong nationwide support among Filipinos for defending the country’s territory and sovereign rights, including the ...

item.title: Online backlash emerges over UFC stage design for White House Freedom 250 event
item.description: A UFC stage design set up for an upcoming event at the White House has sparked widespread online criticism and ridicule. The UFC Freedom 250 stage is being constructed on the South Lawn in Washington,...

item.title: Rare 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster Showcases Pre-War Automotive Design
item.description: The article highlights the elegance and historical significance of the 1935 Bugatti Type 57 ‘Grand Raid’ Roadster, one of the most admired luxury sports cars from the pre-war automotive era. Designed ...

item.title: Florrie Holmes and the Forty Elephants: Women of London’s Notorious All-Female Crime Gang
item.description: The article presents a historical portrait of Florrie Holmes, one of the known members of the Forty Elephants, an all-female criminal gang that operated in London between 1873 and the 1950s. Based in ...

item.title: Antonelli wins Canadian Grand Prix as Russell retires after Mercedes power unit failure
item.description: Kimi Antonelli secured a dominant victory at the Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix after Mercedes teammate George Russell retired from the race due to a power unit failure. The race at Circuit Gilles Vill...

item.title: KBA Opposes Proposed 16 Percent VAT on Mobile Money Fees Over Fears of Reduced Digital Transactions
item.description: The Kenya Bankers Association (KBA) has opposed a proposal in the Finance Bill 2026 that seeks to introduce a 16 percent VAT on charges imposed by mobile money and digital payment platforms such as M-...

item.title: Early Christmas Cover Art from The New Yorker in the 1920s and 1930s
item.description: The article presents a retrospective look at 51 Christmas-themed covers from The New Yorker magazine, focusing on works produced during the 1920s and 1930s. Founded in 1925 by Harold Ross and Jane Gra...

item.title: U.S. Military Targets Iranian Missile Facilities and Boats Near Strait of Hormuz Following Attacks on Naval Ships
item.description: The United States carried out military strikes against Iranian facilities near Bandar Abbas after Iranian forces allegedly attacked U.S. warships operating in the Strait of Hormuz. According to report...

item.title: White House Prepares Large UFC Event Amid Ongoing Construction and Concerns About Insects
item.description: Preparations for the upcoming UFC Freedom 250 event at the White House have drawn attention as construction activity expands across the grounds ahead of the celebration planned for June 14, 2026. Acco...

item.title: A Look Back at Nastassja Kinski’s International Film Career in the 1970s and 1980s
item.description: The article highlights the career and cultural impact of German actress and former model Nastassja Kinski, who became one of the most recognizable international film stars of the late 1970s and 1980s....

item.title: Frank Sinatra’s Early Life and Childhood in Hoboken, New Jersey
item.description: The article explores the early life and childhood of Frank Sinatra, one of the most influential American singers of the 20th century, through rare photographs and biographical details. Sinatra was bor...
